title: Ziad Shanab
content:
People.ContactEmail zshanab@mccarthy.ca
- Université de Montréal
Ziad Shanab is an associate in our Montreal Labour and Employment group. His practice focuses on representing both provincially and federally regulated employers.
Ziad counsels clients about various employment issues, including hirings, disciplinary measures, human rights issues and key terminations, as well as the interpretation and application of employment contracts. He regularly provides employment law support on commercial transactions and advises on questions regarding benefits, major class action risks, executive compensation, workplace policies and termination packages.
With a keen interest in litigation, he also advocates for clients before a range of judicial and administrative bodies, including the Tribunal administratif du travail , the Canadian Industrial Relations Board, arbitration tribunals, and the Superior Court. His practice encompasses cases involving wrongful dismissal, harassment, discrimination, and employment-related class actions.
Prior to joining McCarthy Tetrault, Ziad acted as legal counsel to a major Canadian financial institution, where he regularly advised C-level executives and provided key strategic advice on contentious as well as non-contentious matters, including matters pertaining to employment standards legislation and complaints before the Canadian Human Rights Commission.
Ziad holds a bachelor’s degree in civil law (L.L.B.) as well as a Juris Doctor (J.D.) in North American Common law from University of Montreal.
He was called to the Québec Bar in 2021 and has been a member of the Young Bar of Montreal’s Relations Committee since then.
